violations.description: Tier 1 unit test sleeps Task.Delay(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(17)) to wait for hosted-service initial-delay timer; persistent regression since 2026-04-18 review.
violations.suggestion: Refactor DaprHealthHistoryCollector to accept an injectable TimeProvider (or ISystemClock / Func<TimeSpan,Task> delay) so tests can advance virtual time via FakeTimeProvider — eliminate the 17s wall-clock wait entirely.
violations.code_snippet: await collector.StartAsync(cts.Token); await Task.Delay(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(17)); // 15s delay + 2s buffer

violations.description: Second 17-second Task.Delay in same Tier 1 file (ExecuteAsync_SkipsWrite_WhenNoComponentsReturned).
violations.suggestion: Same TimeProvider injection as line 71 — or convert to a deterministic CancellationToken-driven trigger inside the collector that the test can fire synchronously.

violations.description: Third 17-second Task.Delay in same Tier 1 file (ExecuteAsync_ContinuesOnWriteFailure). Combined cost: 3 x 17s = 51s of CI time per Tier 1 run for one collector class.
violations.suggestion: All three tests should share a TimeProvider abstraction; use Microsoft.Extensions.Time.Testing.FakeTimeProvider to advance time without sleeping.

violations.description: Nine IntegrationTests classes (CommandsControllerTests, CommandRoutingIntegrationTests, CommandStatusIntegrationTests, ConcurrencyConflictIntegrationTests, MultiTenantRoutingIntegrationTests, OpenApiIntegrationTests, ReplayIntegrationTests, ServiceUnavailableIntegrationTests, ValidationTests) use IClassFixture<JwtAuthenticatedWebApplicationFactory>, so each spins its own WebApplicationFactory instead of sharing one via ICollectionFixture.
violations.suggestion: Introduce [CollectionDefinition("JwtAuthFactory")] : ICollectionFixture<JwtAuthenticatedWebApplicationFactory> and decorate the 9 classes with [Collection("JwtAuthFactory")] — one factory boot serves all 9 classes.

violations.description: Status-poll loop uses Task.Delay(500ms) — 500ms increments inside a Tier 3 polling loop is coarser than the 250ms used elsewhere in IntegrationTests; tightening would shorten happy-path latency.
violations.suggestion: Reduce to 250ms (or use the s_statusPollInterval constant pattern adopted by sibling files like CommandLifecycleTests).

violations.description: Many near-identical Facts of the form Local*AccessControlYaml_IsValidYaml_ParsesCorrectly / Production*AccessControlYaml_IsValidYaml_ParsesCorrectly mirror the same shape across 6+ environment+component pairs.
violations.suggestion: Convert to [Theory]/[InlineData("Local", "event-store")] / [InlineData("Production", "event-store")] etc. Cuts test-launch overhead and reduces 22 method signatures to 1.

violations.description: 22 [Fact] methods, zero [Theory]/[InlineData] in a 934-line file — multiplies xUnit per-test launch overhead.
violations.suggestion: Audit for parameterizable groups (e.g., checkpoint count thresholds, retry counts, timeouts) and consolidate via [Theory]/[InlineData].

violations.description: Thread.Sleep(50) inside file-write retry loop — minor performance impact (~250ms worst case across 5 attempts) but blocks the test thread; flagged in determinism review for synchronous-blocking concern.
violations.suggestion: Replace with await Task.Delay(50) and convert TryWriteFaultFile to async; also bound retry budget instead of fixed iteration count.

violations.description: Second Thread.Sleep(50) in TryDeleteFaultFile retry loop — same characteristics as line 244.
violations.suggestion: Same as line 244 — convert to async Task.Delay.

violations.description: 25+ [Fact] methods following AuthorizationBehavior_<scenario>_<expectation> naming with shared arrange shape; some logically parametrizable (matching/wrong/missing claim variations).
violations.suggestion: Group the matching/wrong/missing-claim triples (Domain, Permission, Tenant) into [Theory]/[InlineData] cases. Marginal CI-time win but improves maintainability.

performance_metrics.notes: Wall-clock waste is concentrated in DaprHealthHistoryCollectorTests (3 x 17s = 51s per Tier 1 run, persistent regression). Fixture reuse is otherwise solid: Server.Tests Tier 2 correctly shares one DAPR sidecar via ICollectionFixture<DaprTestContainerFixture>; IntegrationTests Aspire/Keycloak/PubSub use shared collection fixtures; the only fixture-sharing miss is JwtAuthenticatedWebApplicationFactory used per-class across 9 IntegrationTests files.

recommendations: Highest leverage: refactor DaprHealthHistoryCollector to accept TimeProvider (Microsoft.Extensions.Time.Testing.FakeTimeProvider in tests) and remove the 3 x 17s real-time waits — recovers 51s per Tier 1 CI run and eliminates a persistent regression flagged by two prior reviews.

Convert the 9 IntegrationTests/EventStore/*Tests classes from IClassFixture<JwtAuthenticatedWebApplicationFactory> to a shared [CollectionDefinition]/ICollectionFixture pattern — recovers ~10-15s per Tier 3 run by avoiding 8 redundant WebApplicationFactory boots.

Apply [Theory]/[InlineData] consolidation to AccessControlPolicyTests (Local* / Production* mirrors) and ProjectionCheckpointTrackerTests — reduces xUnit per-test overhead and improves maintainability without changing semantics.
summary: Performance is dominated by one persistent regression: 3 x 17-second Task.Delay calls in DaprHealthHistoryCollectorTests (Tier 1) cost 51 wall-clock seconds per CI run and have survived two prior reviews. Fixture reuse is otherwise good — Tier 2 DAPR sidecar is properly shared across 11+ classes via ICollectionFixture, with the only material miss being JwtAuthenticatedWebApplicationFactory rebuilt per class across 9 IntegrationTests files.
